LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PACIFIC COAST ELEVATOR CORPORATION DBA AMTECH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO JULY 9, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 ON THE COURT'S MOTION. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: L. FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-04-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
AMENDED APP FOR LIEN FOR REIMBURESEMENT FILED BY CLAIMANT TRAVELERS CASUALTY AND SURETY COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT PACIFIC COAST ELEVATOR CORPORATION DBA AMTECH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - THE COURT DENIED THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION. DEFENDANT MAINTAINED ITS INITIAL SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN BY RELYING ON PLAINTIFF'S FACTUALLY DEVOID SPECIAL INTERROGATORY RESPONSES. IN OPPOSITION, THE DECLARATION OF MR. GERK IS ADMISSIBLE TO ESTABLISH WHAT QUALIFIES AS DUE DILIGENCE AND WHAT CUSTOMARY DUTIES DEFENDANT OWED. PLAINTIFF CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER DEFENDANT BREACHED ITS DUTY AND ACTED NEGLIGENTLY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-09-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Oct-25-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PACIFIC COAST ELEVATOR CORPORATION DBA AMTECH ELEVATOR SERVICES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF AND THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR EXPRESS CONTRACTUAL DEFENSE AND INDEMNITY IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT OF AMTECH ELEVATOR SERVICES AGAINST CROSS-DEFENDANT ED MITCHELL WEST, LLC.

Appearances: Joseph W. Strella, Jr. of Clapp Moroney Bellagamba Vucinich Beeman Scheley (650.989.5400) for cross-defendant and responding party; Gary J. Nevolo of Tucker Ellis LLP (415.617.2209) for cross-complainant and moving party.

Argued and the Court rules as follows: Cross-Complainant Amtech Elevator Services' motion for summary adjudication as to the second and third causes of action for declaratory relief and express contractual indemnity is denied as a matter of law. In a noninsurance indemnity agreement, the language on point must be particularly clear and explicit, and will be construed strictly against the indemnitee. (See Crawford v. Weather Shield Mfg. Inc. (2008) 44 Cal.4th 541, 553.) Construing the non-insurance contract against Amtech, the language in the agreement is not particularly clear and explicit to require Cross-Defendant Ed Mitchell West, LLC to indemnify and defend Amtech against third party claims arising from a breakdown or malfunction of the elevator. (See Declaration of Thiry in Support of Amtech's Motion for Summary Adjudication ("Thiry Decl."), Exh. A.) According to the plain language of the Agreement, Cross-Defendant's duty to indemnify Amtech is not embraced by the matters in Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint as it does not arise from personal injury or property damage caused by negligent acts or omissions during the performance of work. (See Civ. Code 2778(4); Thiry Decl., Exh. A.) Even if the two paragraphs in the Agreement were read in conjunction, the language should be interpreted to mean that Amtech intended to "not be bothered" by Ed Mitchell by claims arising during performance, and/or the malfunction of the elevator. (See Queen Villas Homeowners Association v. TCB Property Management (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 1, 15.) Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR #6185 (302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KHALDOUN A. BAGHDADI OF THE LAW OFFICES OF WALKUP MELODIA & SCHOENBERGER, TEL. NO. 981-7210 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. GARY J. NEVOLO OF TUCKER ELLIS LLP, TEL. NO. 617-2209 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT PACIFIC COAST ELEVATOR;
ATTY. ANDREW S. WERNER OF THE LAW OFFICES OF CESARI, WERNER AND MORIARTY, TEL. NO. 391-1113 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS WB 375 SUTTER ST & CALLAHAN PROPERTY.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PACIFIC COAST ELEVATOR CORPORATION DBA AMTECH's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - The Court denied the motion for summary judgment/adjudication. Defendant maintained its initial summary judgment burden by relying on plaintiff's factually devoid special interrogatory responses. In opposition, the declaration of Mr. Gerk is admissible to establish what qualifies as due diligence and what customary duties defendant owed. Plaintiff creates a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant breached its duty and acted negligently. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)
